==Pokémon==
<!--Magnemite and Magneton were pure Electric in Gen I-->
{{MissingInfo|2|enva|java}}
{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Ethan
|pkmn=Magneton
|nick=Number 6
|type1=Electric
|img=Ethan Number Six Magnemite.png
|img2=Ethan Number Six.png
|epnum=EP101
|epname=Get Along, Little Pokémon
|vaen=Eric Stuart
|desc='''Number 6''' (Japanese: '''6番''' ''No. 6'') is one of Ethan's Pokémon that is used to store and power local towns with electricity, but it was not loyal like the others. It is identifiable due to its uniquely bent magnets. It first appeared as a {{p|Magnemite}} with the group in Ethan's care. When they were set free to relax, all of the Magnemite went off in different directions. But later, when the Magnemite were all rallied back together thanks to Ethan's {{p|Jolteon}} which used its electricity to attract them, Number 6 wandered off, but was soon corralled by Jolteon. Ethan explains that Number 6 is the "black sheep" and frequently wanders off.

Later, after Ethan is hurt by {{TRT}}, {{Ash}} is tasked with bringing the Magnemite to the next town. However, none of the Magnemite obey Ash and wander off. {{AP|Pikachu}} manages to do Jolteon's job and brings all the Magnemite back, except Number 6. Ash finds it near a stream but {{TRT}} is also there. They use static electricity to pull all of the Magnemite, including Number 6, which Ash is still holding onto. Pikachu attempts to corral the Magnemite back from Team Rocket, but the power of the static is too great. Seeing how hard Pikachu is fighting for the Magnemite, Number 6 collides with Team Rocket's electric ray and Pikachu's Thunderbolt absorbing both and freeing its fellow Magnemite. Suddenly it [[Evolution|evolves]] into a {{p|Magneton}} and sends Team Rocket blasting off.

Number 6's known moves are {{m|Thunder Shock}} and {{m|Thunderbolt}}.}} 

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Ethan
|pkmn=Magnemite
|nick=Magnemite (multiple)
|type1=Electric
|img=Ethan Magnemite.png
|cap=Ethan's Magnemite
|epnum=EP101
|epname=Get Along, Little Pokémon
|vaen=Eric Stuart
|desc=Ethan owns multiple {{p|Magnemite}} which absorb lightning from storms, and supply electricity to local towns. They are named using numbers. They first appeared when they were absorbing electricity from a storm, nearly zapping {{Ash}} and {{ashfr}}. They took a liking to [[Ash's Pikachu]], smothering it and zapping Ash in the process. They are later seen absorbing another lighting storm in the night. The next day they are caught by {{TRT}} using a giant magnet, but with the help of Ash's Pikachu, they are saved. When they scatter about, {{p|Jolteon}} corrals the group, but when it is hurt, Pikachu takes over. They are all very loyal to Ethan except one, '''Number 6'''.

Later, they are captured again by Team Rocket using static electricity, but are soon freed by Number 6.

Magnemite's known moves are {{m|Thunder Shock}} and {{m|Thunderbolt}}.}} 

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Ethan
|pkmn=Jolteon
|type1=Electric
|img=Ethan_Jolteon.png
|epnum=EP101
|epname=Get Along, Little Pokémon
|vajp=Katsuyuki Konishi
|vaen=Eric Stuart
|desc=Ethan's {{p|Jolteon}} herd Ethan's {{p|Magnemite}}. It first appeared herding Magnemite which were absorbing electricity from a lightning storm. The next day Jolteon is captured along with {{AP|Pikachu}} by {{TRT}}. Jolteon then uses {{m|Pin Missile}}, successfully setting it and Pikachu free. It then tries to control the group of Magnemite with a {{m|Thunder Wave}} and frees the Magnemite, but while focusing on freeing the Magnemite, it is attacked and hurt by [[Jessie's Arbok]]. When Ash goes on ahead with the Magnemite, due to its injury, Jolteon stays behind.

Jolteon's known moves are {{m|Pin Missile}} and {{m|Thunder Wave}}.}} 

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Ethan
|pkmn=Tauros
|nick=Tauros (×2)
|type1=Normal
|gender=male
|img=Ethan_Tauros.png
|cap=Ethan's Tauros
|epnum=EP101
|epname=Get Along, Little Pokémon
|desc=Ethan has two {{p|Tauros}}, which pull his stagecoach so he can traverse [[Mandarin Island South]]. They first appeared giving {{Ash}} and {{ashfr}} shelter from the {{p|Magnemite}}. After the attack by {{TRT}}, one stays with the wagon, while the other is ridden by Ash as he travels to the next town with Ethan's Magnemite. 

None of Tauros's moves are known.}}
